howItWorks.intro: This required minimum distribution calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: RMD Formula
rules.formula: RMD = Account Balance / Life Expectancy Divisor
rules.explanation: Divide your prior year-end balance by the IRS Uniform Lifetime Table factor for your age.

howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.a: Under SECURE 2.0 Act, RMDs begin at age 73 for those born 1951-1959, and age 75 for those born 1960 or later. Roth IRAs do not require RMDs during the owner's lifetime.

faq.q: What happens if I miss an RMD?
faq.a: The penalty for missing an RMD was reduced from 50% to 25% of the shortfall under SECURE 2.0. If corrected within 2 years, the penalty drops to 10%. Always take your full RMD by December 31.
